skill.name: when-documenting-code-use-doc-generator
skill.description: Generates project documentation, API references, README files, coverage reports, and diagram guidance from an existing codebase.
skill.summary: Generate practical codebase documentation from repository structure, routes, metadata, and documentation gaps.
skill.author: DNYoussef

security_audit.summary: Static analysis found many command, network, filesystem, credential, and weak-crypto patterns. Review shows no confirmed malicious intent or prompt-injection attempt, but the executable shell scripts run external commands, invoke a third-party npm package with npx, and create or overwrite documentation files. Publish with a warning that users should review scripts before execution and run them in a trusted repository.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party Command Execution Through npx
medium_findings.description: The slash-command scripts invoke npx claude-flow@alpha hooks and memory commands. This is legitimate for the documented workflow, but it can download and execute third-party npm package code in the user repository, so users should review and pin dependencies before running it.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx invocations are direct and appear intentional. I did not find evidence that they exfiltrate secrets, but running an alpha package from npm is a real execution risk.

medium_findings.title: Repository File Modification
medium_findings.description: The scripts create docs directories, generate API files, back up README.md, and write a new README.md. This matches the skill purpose, but it can alter project files and should be run only after review or with version control available.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit and required for generated documentation. The risk is operational data loss or unwanted changes, not confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Broad Repository Scanning With Shell Pipelines
medium_findings.description: The scripts scan source trees with find, grep, xargs, jq, git, and python commands to infer routes and metadata. This is expected for documentation generation, but shell parsing and broad scans can behave unexpectedly on unusual paths or large repositories.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and serve a documentation purpose. The concern is moderate because the pipelines operate on repository content and are not robustly sandboxed.

low_findings.title: Network and Credential References Are Mostly Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Hardcoded URLs, fetch examples, curl examples, DATABASE_URL, JWT_SECRET, and GitHub token references appear in generated documentation templates or CI examples. I did not find evidence that these values are read from the environment and sent to an external endpoint by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are examples or generated Markdown content. They do not show secret collection or network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography and reconnaissance detections appear to match words inside documentation, diagrams, URLs, timestamps, examples, or generated text. No evidence found of cryptographic operations, password hashing, host scanning, or network reconnaissance logic.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual context shows documentation and Graphviz labels rather than crypto or reconnaissance code. The static analyzer likely matched generic tokens.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned External Tool Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The scripts call npx claude-flow@alpha. This pattern can execute package code that changes over time and should be pinned or replaced with a reviewed local dependency for safer marketplace use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is directly present in all three scripts. It is risky even without proof of malicious intent because runtime package contents may change.

dangerous_patterns.title: Direct Documentation File Writes
dangerous_patterns.description: The README and API scripts write generated content directly into project documentation paths. Users should run in a clean working tree and review diffs before committing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The write operations are direct and clear. They are expected behavior, but still a dangerous pattern for automated marketplace skills.

content.value_statement: Keeping project documentation current is hard when APIs, routes, and setup steps change often. This skill generates README content, API references, documentation reports, and diagram guidance from the codebase.
actual_capabilities: Scans repository files to detect project type, routes, functions, and documentation gaps.
Generates OpenAPI-style API documentation and a Markdown API reference.
Creates or updates README.md content with setup, usage, testing, deployment, and license sections.
Produces a documentation coverage report for JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python source files.
Provides process guidance for architecture diagrams using Graphviz and Mermaid patterns.
limitations: The shell scripts use simple grep-based analysis, not full language-aware AST parsing.
The inline documentation script reports and counts targets but does not fully insert complete custom comments.
Generated API and README content may include placeholders that require human review.
Scripts can write files in the current repository, so review changes before committing.

use_cases.title: Document a Small API Service
use_cases.description: Generate an initial API reference, README, and documentation report for an Express, FastAPI, or NestJS project.

use_cases.title: Improve Project Onboarding
use_cases.description: Create a structured README with setup, usage, testing, deployment, configuration, and support sections.

use_cases.title: Audit Documentation Coverage
use_cases.description: Scan JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python files to identify public functions that need documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Basic Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: create a first documentation pass.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate project documentation for this repository. Include a README outline, API documentation plan, and any documentation gaps you find.

prompt_templates.title: Create API Reference
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the API routes in this project and generate an OpenAPI-style reference with endpoints, parameters, responses, and authentication notes.

prompt_templates.title: Update README for Onboarding
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a README update for this project. Include prerequisites, installation, usage, testing, deployment, configuration, support, and license sections.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Complete Documentation System
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a complete documentation workflow for this repository. Include generated files, coverage checks, diagram outputs, review steps, and CI validation.

output_examples.input: A Node.js API project with Express routes and no current API reference.
output: An API reference with health check, authentication, response format, error handling, and rate limit sections.
An OpenAPI-style specification file under the docs directory.
A summary of detected route count and project framework.

output_examples.input: A repository with an outdated README and missing setup instructions.
output: A regenerated README with prerequisites, installation, usage, documentation links, development commands, testing, deployment, and configuration sections.
A backup of the existing README when overwrite mode is not enabled.

output_examples.input: A mixed JavaScript, TypeScript, and Python codebase with sparse comments.
output: A documentation coverage report listing total files, total functions, undocumented functions, and recommended next steps.
Guidance on JSDoc and Python docstring standards for public functions.

best_practices: Run documentation scripts in a clean git working tree and review the generated diff.
Replace placeholder values, sample URLs, and generic descriptions before publishing documentation.
Pin or locally review external tooling before running npx-based commands in sensitive repositories.
anti_patterns: Do not publish generated README or API docs without checking placeholders and examples.
Do not run the scripts against repositories that contain secrets unless the environment is trusted.
Do not treat grep-based route and function detection as complete documentation coverage.

faq.question: Does this skill generate API documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It can create an OpenAPI-style YAML file and a Markdown API reference for common API project patterns.

faq.question: Can it overwrite my README.md?
faq.answer: Yes. The README script writes README.md and creates a timestamped backup when overwrite mode is not enabled.

faq.question: Does it support Python projects?
faq.answer: Yes. It detects pyproject.toml metadata and scans Python functions for documentation coverage.

faq.question: Does it add final inline comments automatically?
faq.answer: Not fully. The inline script identifies targets and generates a report, but custom comments still need review and completion.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill includes executable shell scripts, repository file writes, and npx calls to a third-party alpha package.

faq.question: Is there evidence of malicious behavior?
faq.answer: No confirmed malicious intent was found. Most token, URL, and credential references are documentation examples.
